calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-07-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Neil Rones appeared by telephone for and with Jasmine Powell by telephone.
Alice Keith was also on the telephone with Jasmine.

The Court noted that the issue of unauthorized distributions is the same issue that was recently discussed in another case with Mr. Rones. Mr. Rones stated that he provides the same cautious language to every Administrator he has and that he can't explain why this happened. Ms. Powell stated that she was under the impression from the title company that the process was over, that the distributions were made before they were working closely with Mr. Rones, that when the distributions happened her and her Grandma were in the process of refinancing and that she believes the distributions were done at the end of 2018. The Court noted that Ms. Powell signed the document titled "Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative" which indicates that she was aware that she was not to spend any of the estate's money without the Court's permission. Ms. Powell stated that the Court is correct, that she has a lot of confusion with this process, that to her knowledge the funds were her Grandma's and that she followed the instructions that were provided by her Grandma. Mr. Rones stated that when they started this probate the title company ran a search on the property, that the title company required that the three estates be probated in order to provide title insurance on the property, and that he went to three title companies and that they all required probate of the three estates. Mr. Rones also stated that if they had not followed the instructions to probate all three estates, the property would've been foreclosed, that he had to provide three orders to the title company, that there were two probates for Annie Bell Dunne, that the same property popped up on another probate and that they didn't have any choice. In addition, Mr. Rones stated that one third of the sale proceeds went to this estate, that he was not comfortable pulling it back out under Probate Code 12251, that the agreement of the administration was that he was to take his time to straighten everything out and that most of the time spent was with working with the title company. The Court stated that it is not inclined to grant extraordinary fees, that the information that was received regarding the estate fees was not persuasive and that it is not inclined to continue this again. Ms. Keith stated that she authorized her Granddaughter to do everything for her. The Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-31-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Neil A. Rones, for Petitioner Jasmine Powell.

In response to an inquiry from the Court as to the cause for delay in bringing the petition for probate, Mr. Rones stated that none of in the family consulted an attorney at the time of the death of the decedent, so they did not know of the need for probating the estate. He said that the Petitioner Jasmine Powell was now seeking appointment with full powers, and that waivers of bond had been submitted by all heirs and filed with the original petition. The Court granted the petition, subject to review of the order. A status hearing re closing of estate was set for June 24, 2020, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Anasse. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
